output: Implemented batch 1. T1 now invalidates active shot queries after shot creation, and T2 now persists final-video dismissals across remounts while suppressing the badge when the final video is already on the timeline. Relevant Vitest modules passed, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-final-video-20260406-0959/execution_batch_1.json`.
deviations: Advisory observation mismatch: executor claimed files not observed in git status/content hash delta: .megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-final-video-20260406-0959/execution_batch_1.json
Advisory audit finding: Git status shows changed files not claimed by any task: .megaplan/debt.json, .megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-final-video-20260406-0959
Advisory audit finding: Sense check SC3 is missing an executor acknowledgment.
Advisory audit finding: Sense check SC4 is missing an executor acknowledgment.

task_updates.executor_notes: Removed the inactive-only invalidation filter from both shot creation paths. Verified the active ShotsContext query uses the same ['shots', projectId, maxImagesPerShot] prefix that invalidateShotsQueries targets, so invalidateQueries now refetches the mounted list query as intended.

Moved final-video dismissal state to a module-level Set keyed by finalVideo.id and rewired TimelineEditor to suppress badges when a current row clip already references that final video generation. Verified the switch chain remains consistent: useSwitchToFinalVideo registers generationId=finalVideo.id, stores the asset on the inserted clip meta, and the new rows->meta->registry scan detects it.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed by inspection: ShotsContext mounts useListShots with queryKey ['shots', projectId, maxImagesPerShot], and invalidateShotsQueries invalidates ['shots', projectId] without a refetchType override. TanStack Query will therefore mark the active shots list stale and refetch it.

Confirmed by tracing the code path: useSwitchToFinalVideo creates the replacement asset with generationId: finalVideo.id, stores that asset key on the new clip's meta entry, and resolveFinalVideoShotIds now scans rows -> meta[action.id].asset -> registry.assets[assetKey].generationId, so a placed final video is detected from actual timeline clips.
